How did we go from a glut but not a glut so quickly? It's all about the trading cycle of crude and I call it The Asian Crude Buying Conundrum. 

Read the slide for more details because a picture speaks a thousand words.

#oott

quotedTweet.text:
Products E/W has notched up higher since the escalations restarted. 

It all boils down to the Asian Crude Buying Conundrum. 

In summary, Asia might be underbought for September and runrates may need to come off even with whatever spectacular cracks we may be seeing.

Have a look at the slide and see if it answers your questions. 

#oott

text: Freight: The paper market has not yet priced what the fundamentals are pointing to. Are you listening, guys? #oott

quotedTweet.text: TD20 WAF Suezmax. Bonny Light crude RBI at -$5.36/bbl, confirming Nigerian barrels are cheap into NWE. Freight RBI at -$7.78/mt, confirming TD20 rates are undervalued relative to global competition. Both signals firmly bullish. WAF crude attractiveness into global destinations has been improving for two weeks. August paper firmed from WS 220 to WS 225 yesterday. September traded from WS 190 to WS 205. Q4 from WS 185 to WS 190. The bigger story is the Bab-el-Mandeb risk building in the background. Houthi attacks on the strait would reroute Yanbu-loading Suezmaxes via the Cape, increasing voyage distance by 2.5 times to the Far East and absorbing significant global Suezmax supply. That displacement tightens the WAF list and pushes TD20 higher. Enquiry has been slow this week and spot could drift near term, but the risk is skewed to the upside. Owners: hold firm. The paper market has not yet priced what the fundamentals are pointing to.

text:
VLSFO's tight, alright

Haven't forgotten you, the unloved barrel. 

You're in short supply now with the medium crude trapped again in the Middle East. Demand for 0.5% fuel oil is strong too - on top of bunkers, we need you for power gen for this hot summer season too!

Doesn't help that arbs are still shut from the West, even with 0.5% E/W so high. 

I think you are headed for more strength until the crude supply situation eases.

#oott

Refining constraints basically pushed product cracks to extreme whilst crude prices remain fairly muted.

I think we are close to that turning point now, with CPC halting and Bab el-Mandeb at risk of closure too.

Europe’s gas storage target is slipping out of reach

Europe is now more than half-way through the summer replenishment season and the region is falling further and further behind rebuilding gas inventories ready for next winter.  

Underground storage sites across the European Union are on average just 54% full, the second lowest on record, and far below the average fill rate of 70% at this point in the previous ten years. 

The European Union’s target of refilling storage sites to an average of 90% before the end of November is rapidly slipping out of reach. 

EU regulations provide some flexibility to reduce the storage target to 80% or even 75% and it looks increasingly likely that will be needed ...

Strait of Hormuz over the last 72 hours: escalation continues.

Visible vessel traffic fell off a cliff. Only the Iran lane has vessels with AIS on, nothing in the Oman lane. We know there were still transits because UKMTO reported vessels being attacked and IRGC said 4 vessels were attacked yesterday. One tanker, Kavomaleas, was hit and caught on fire. You can see that in the video time-lapse. 

The Oman lane will remain heavily contested. Bloomberg reported that tanker owners are offering 6 months' worth of pay if the crews make the voyage in and out of the Strait of Hormuz. How much is your life worth? 

I guess we will see how long this continues, but what's obvious is that this conflict remains all-or-nothing. 

Video from @MarineTraffic

Brazil’s billionaire Batista brothers have acquired a stake in an oil production project in #Venezuela, the latest deal to emerge in a country that the Trump administration is vowing to reinvigorate after years of sanctions.

Fluxus Oil, Gas & Energy, owned by Joesley and Wesley Batista, acquired A&B Oil and Gas in recent months, according to people familiar with the transaction. A&B owns a 49% stake in the Petrolera Roraima joint venture with Venezuela’s national oil company Petroleos de Venezuela SA, said the people, who weren’t authorized to discuss the information. The terms weren’t disclosed.

The United States has informed Israel that it plans to escalate its strikes on Iran in the coming days, including the use of heavy bombers for the first time since this year’s joint US-Israeli campaign against the Islamic Republic, the Kan public broadcaster reports.

According to the report, the bombers are expected to target the Pickaxe Mountain nuclear site near Qom, based on repeated threats by US President Donald Trump over the past week that the area would be hit “pretty soon.”

The close security coordination between Jerusalem and Washington is reportedly intended to allow Israel to prepare for the possibility that Iran could respond to the intensified US strikes by escalating its own attacks, including launching missiles at Israel.

Israel is at its peak level of readiness tonight following the US update, Kan reports.

Stay on high alert here. Every time this clown starts puffing his chest and running his mouth, it’s almost always a setup for a classic TACO pivot down the road. 

That is literally Page 1 of the TACO playbook we have watched a million times. Never buy a single word until he actually puts execution behind it.

If anything, the market fears his silence far more, and oil prices love when he stays quiet. Regardless of whether it comes off as bullish or bearish, NASDAQ would much rather see him step in with some verbal intervention.

#oott #iran

Not looking to pump the hype train here since this is just initial observation, but the setup is a little shifting.

The optical frame from July 19th showed zero tanker traffic at Jebel Dhanna Terminal, and today's SAR pass confirms still no tankers in sight. All three SPMs are sitting bone-dry.

Zirku Island Terminal is printing empty on today's SAR pass too. Friendly reminder: Jebel Dhanna is the home terminal for Murban, and Zirku is the flagship outlet for Upper Zakum.

Seeing zero loading activity across both hubs is a rare print—probably the first time we've seen this since the MoU was inked. Could just be STS run rates slowing post-Sinokor tanker attak, but then again i'm just talking out of my ass here.

This might explain why Abu Dhabi was quietly paring down its tender volumes for next month. I’ll keep a tight watch on the feeds and keep you posted.

#oott #iran

Sorry to keep pumping the tires on real gold and digital gold.

But look—the USD long was insanely over-crowded, while precious metals and BTC were sitting as total red-headed stepchildren of the mkt.

While gold fell a tad short of the mark, the positioning flush across the board was executed like a charm. BTC and platinum have completely cleared out the junk!

Even with momentum factors getting crushed and market darlings taking a heavy beating, these are holding up amazingly well on the downside and delivering sweet alpha.

Macro trajectory is still up in the air, so this tape could turn on us anytime, but that’s the risk budget you have to commit. As of right now, the thesis is hitting all the right notes.

#gold $btc
